title: ipv6_addr_to_str does not use zero compression
description:
ipv6_addr.c:145-155 formats IPv6 addresses without RFC 5952 zero compression. A link-local like fe80::1234:5678:abcd:ef01 is printed as fe80:0000:0000:0000:1234:5678:abcd:ef01 (39 chars).

What goes wrong as-is: Log messages are harder to read, copy-paste into other tools may confuse users expecting standard format. For LICHEN's link-local addresses (fe80:: prefix with 4 zero hextets), this is noticeable.

Note: Implementing RFC 5952 compression is more code and not urgent. If a fix is desired, Zephyr provides net_addr_ntop() when CONFIG_NET_IPV6 is enabled, which already implements compression.
close_reason: Won't fix: zero compression is nice-to-have, current format is valid IPv6
